overview: BG Yawgmoth is a complex, synergistic combo deck in Modern that loops Yawgmoth, Thran Physician with Undying creatures to draw cards and win via sacrifice triggers. The deck relies on mana dorks, tutors, and careful resource management to navigate a steep learning curve and an interactive metagame. Mastering its sequencing allows players to consistently execute the combo or apply alternate win conditions.

keep_criteria: Hands containing mana dorks like Birds of Paradise or Ignoble Hierarch
Low-cost Undying creatures to start the loop
Tutors like Chord of Calling or Eldritch Evolution
mulligan_criteria: Hands without combo pieces or tutors
Hands vulnerable to early disruption without interaction

examples.reason: Contains mana dorks, a tutor, and core combo pieces to establish early game presence and search for missing links.

early_game: Play mana dorks like Birds of Paradise and Ignoble Hierarch to accelerate mana
Use Wall of Roots to generate mana on both turns
Establish low-cost Undying creatures to enable the loop
mid_game: Deploy tutors like Chord of Calling and Eldritch Evolution to find combo pieces
Begin sacrificing creatures to trigger Yawgmoth, Thran Physician and draw cards
Navigate opponent's interaction by playing around counterspells and discards
late_game: Execute the full Undying/Yawgmoth loop to drain opponent's life or draw into victory
Utilize alternate win conditions like Hapatra's tokens or Grist's abilities
Manage resources to create constant combo attempts that evade answers

key_cards.name: Yawgmoth, Thran Physician
key_cards.notes: Draws cards when creatures with +1/+1 counters gain -1/-1 counters, enabling the Undying loop.

key_cards.name: Blood Artist
key_cards.notes: Drains opponent's life whenever a creature dies, synergizing with the sacrifice loop.

key_cards.name: Geralf's Messenger
key_cards.notes: Drains opponent's life upon entering the battlefield, repeatedly triggered by the Undying loop.

key_cards.name: Birds of Paradise
key_cards.notes: Classic mana acceleration to cast the 4-mana core piece.

key_cards.name: Ignoble Hierarch
key_cards.notes: New dork from Modern Horizons II that provides mana and tribal synergy.

key_cards.name: Wall of Roots
key_cards.notes: Generates two mana for tutors like Chord of Calling and can be activated on opponent's turn.

key_cards.name: Chord of Calling
key_cards.notes: Reliable tutor for low-cost creatures, fitting the deck's early game focus.

key_cards.name: Eldritch Evolution
key_cards.notes: Functions as a 3-mana tutor to put 4-cost creatures directly into play, highly valuable with Undying cards.

key_cards.name: Thoughtseize
key_cards.notes: Minimal interaction used to remove key threats, though opponents often play around it.

key_cards.name: Grist, the Hunger Tide
key_cards.notes: Creates bodies, destroys creatures/planeswalkers via sacrifice, and can win the game.

sequencing_tips: Identify the best plays and anticipate the opponent's worst answers to play around them.
Control your own sacrifices carefully, as they are an essential resource for the loop.
Expose Yawgmoth, Thran Physician at the safest time, preferably with combo possibilities in response to removal.
Manage resources to either go off quickly with dorks and Eldritch Evolution or create constant combo attempts to evade answers.

matchups.advice: Play around counterspells and discards by sequencing tutors and combo pieces carefully.
Use Outland Liberator as a silver bullet to bypass graveyard restrictions.
Sideboard Outland Liberator to deal with problematic permanents and enable a more interactive approach.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: BG Yawgmoth is a deck that keeps appearing in the meta in a more contained way, but that always shows a lot of efficiency... This is one of the few combos that still stands in the format currently.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: end the game, either with Blood Artist on the battlefield or with Geralf's Messenger
Grist... may even be an alternate wincondition.
Hapatra, Vizier of Poisons is practically an alternate windoncidition

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: identify which are the best plays and always think about the opponent's worst answers and play around them
needing to control your own sacrifices, which are an essential resource
know the safest time to expose Yawgmoth, preferably with combo possibilities in response to some removal
By managing your resources, you can either go off fast, thanks to the dorks and Eldritch Evolution, or create a constant sequence of combo attempts, thus evading interactive decks' answers.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: there are many counterspells and discards in almost every deck
ignores effects like Leyline of Sanctity
good answer against sideboard cards like Graveyard Hates and Torpor Orb
dealing with problematic cards from the current metagame like Urza's Saga, Hammer Time's artifacts and Chalice of the Void
